LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CONNIE FUNG, RONALD FUNG, AND, FUNG TRUST DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACTS THAT GAVE RISE TO THIS COMPLAINT AROSE IN 2006 AND ARE BARRED BY THE ONE-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 340 AND MENEFEE VS. OSTAWRI (1991) 228 CAL.APP. 3D 239. ADDITIONAL ATTEMPTS TO AVOID THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BY DELETING FACTS THAT WOULD RENDER THE COMPLAINT BARRED AND ADDING CONCLUSORY PARAGRAPHS THAT PURPORT TO COLLECT DAMAGES FOR HARM THAT OCCURRED ONLY WITHIN THE STATUTORY PERIOD RENDERS THIS COMPLAINT ADDITIONALLY BARRED BY THE SHAM PLEADING DOCTRINE. (SEE STATE EX REL. METZ VS. CCC INFORMATION SERVICES, INC. (2007) 149 CAL.APP. 4TH 402.) NO. REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT THE DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CONNIE FUNG, RONALD FUNG, and, FUNG TRUST DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Ariel M. Gershon, Esq. of Greenstein & McDonald (415) 773-1240, appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party; Michael TCheng, Esq. of Colombatto Sims LLP (415) 391-6182, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The facts that gave rise to this complaint arose in 2006 and are barred by the one-year statute of limitations pursuant to CCP section 340 and Menefee vs. Ostawri (1991) 228 Cal.App. 3d 239. Additional attempts to avoid the statute of limitations by deleting facts that would render the complaint barred and adding conclusory paragraphs that purport to collect damages for harm that occurred only within the statutory period renders this complaint additionally barred by the sham pleading doctrine. (See State ex rel. Metz vs. CCC Information Services, Inc. (2007) 149 Cal.App. 4th 402.) No. reasonable probability that the defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
